## Headline

All windows start today (2026-06-22) to match Azure's forecast API windows. Bottom-up = **£1.815/day** clean steady-state run-rate × days in window.

| Horizon          | Window              | Bottom-up forecast | Azure forecast | Delta              | Confidence |
|------------------|---------------------|-------------------:|---------------:|-------------------:|------------|
| Rest of month    | 06-22 → 06-30 (9d)  | £16.34             | £11.82         | **+£4.52 (+38%)**  | High       |
| Next 30 days     | 06-22 → 07-22 (30d) | £54.45             | £40.55         | **+£13.90 (+34%)** | Medium     |
| Next quarter     | 06-22 → 09-20 (90d) | £163.35            | £123.72        | **+£39.63 (+32%)** | Low        |

**TL;DR — the marketing websites you shipped today do *not* move the forecast, and Azure still under-forecasts by ~32–38% for the same go-live reason as last week.** Today's marketing-site work (epic tc-w5w9, PRs #599–604) created two `Standard_LRS / Hot` Storage Accounts to hold the SEO snapshot. Dev holds **1.34 MB**; prod is **empty** (cutover pending). Data-at-rest cost is **~£0.00002/month**, transactions are pennies, the Static Web Apps that serve the pages are **Free SKU (£0)**, and the page *render* runs on free GitHub runners — not Azure. Your instinct is right that the regen is **weekly** (`cron 0 4 * * 1`, Mondays), so it's not a daily expense — but the more important point is that **even a daily** regen would be invisible on this bill. The "spike" you imagined today hasn't even landed: Azure's current-day cost data is radically incomplete (06-22 reads £0.30 so far vs the ~£1.82 norm), and when it fills in it'll be the usual run-rate plus a few pennies of one-off blob-seeding/CI churn. The real forecast story is unchanged from 2026-06-18: the steady-state run-rate has settled at **£1.815/day**, Azure's trailing-average model is still dragged down by cheap pre-go-live early-June days (~£1.1/day), so it lands ~£1.31/day and under-shoots by a third. Budget **~£54/next-30-days**, not Azure's ~£40. Calendar-month June will land around **£48**.

## Why Azure's forecast is wrong

Same drivers as last week, now with a full clean 7-day window of evidence. **None of the deltas are marketing-site related** — they are all the mid-June .NET→Go go-live step-change that Azure's linear extrapolation cannot fit.

- **Go-live warm replica — the main thing Azure misses (+£0.25/day, ~£7.5/mo, always-on).** `ca-town-crier-api-go-prod` runs `minReplicas=1` (warm) and costs a steady **£0.251/day** across the clean window. The .NET prod app it replaced was scale-to-zero and cost **£0 on idle days** (June 1–9 show zero Container Apps spend). A continuous cost appeared mid-month that simply isn't in the trailing window Azure averages over. Deliberate, accepted go-live decision — *not* flagged for reversal.

- **Cosmos is the bill, and it's drifting up (+£0.25/day over the month).** `cosmos-town-crier-shared` averages **£1.224/day** over the clean window (~67% of the bill), up from ~£1.00/day in early June. Azure's trailing 30-day average sits below the *current* level and keeps falling behind as the line drifts up.

- **Active polling is continuous (+£0.11/day).** `job-tc-poll-prod` holds a steady **£0.106/day** (negligible in early June). Event-triggered Service Bus polling is busy every cycle.

- **Verified change-points in the actuals timeline:**

  | Date       | Commit / event                                            | Effect on run-rate                                                   |
  |------------|-----------------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------------------------------|
  | 2026-06-14 | `621714c2` Go prod bound + warmed; `.NET` apps stop       | Cutover peak £2.20/day (one-off); .NET → £0 from 06-15               |
  | 2026-06-15 | First clean Go-only steady state                          | £1.82 (15) / £1.81 (16) / £1.79 (17) / £1.70 (18) / £1.84 (19) / £1.93 (20) / £1.81 (21) |
  | 2026-06-22 | `f707f587` per-env SEO Storage Accounts (#599) — **today**| **~£0.00/day** — 1.34 MB dev, empty prod (see §"Marketing websites") |
  | (ongoing)  | Cosmos RU drift                                            | £1.00 → £1.22/day over June                                          |

---

## Marketing websites — the cost of what shipped today

You asked specifically about today's marketing-site launch. Here is the line-by-line, and the answer is **it adds effectively nothing, weekly or daily.**

**What shipped (epic tc-w5w9 / GH #598, PRs #599–604, today):** the SEO/marketing pages (per-authority + per-town planning pages) were decoupled so their content comes from a **Blob snapshot** instead of a per-release API call. That required a new per-environment Storage Account (`f707f587`).

| Component | Resource | Config | Cost |
|-----------|----------|--------|-----:|
| SEO snapshot store (dev) | `sttowncrierdev` | `StorageV2 / Standard_LRS / Hot`, shared-key off, 1 `seo-snapshot` container, **1.34 MB** | ~£0.00002/mo at rest |
| SEO snapshot store (prod) | `sttowncrierprod` | same config, **empty** (prod blob cutover pending, tag-gated → tc-so05) | £0 |
| Page hosting (dev + prod) | `swa-town-crier-dev` / `-prod` | **Free SKU** | £0 |
| Page render | GitHub Actions runners | free (public repo) | £0 (not Azure) |
| Snapshot fetch | weekly call to gated Go API → Cosmos | `cron 0 4 * * 1` (Mondays) | a small weekly Cosmos RU burst, sub-penny |

- **Storage at rest is a rounding error.** 1.34 MB of Hot `Standard_LRS` is **£0.00002/month**. Even if the snapshot grows 100× it's still under a penny.
- **Transactions are pennies.** The snapshot JSON is written once per weekly refresh and read once per CD build. At `Standard_LRS` op rates (~£0.05/10k writes, ~£0.004/10k reads) this is fractions of a penny per month.
- **Today's "spike" is a one-off and hasn't even landed.** PRs #599–604 each ran CI today (blob seeding + several render/deploy iterations), so there *was* a one-off burst of storage transactions and a few extra Cosmos reads during snapshot fetch. But (a) it's pennies, and (b) Azure's current-day cost data is radically incomplete — 06-22 currently reads **£0.30** total (Cosmos £0.02 vs its £1.22 norm), and `sttowncrierdev` reads **£0** so far. The spike will be invisible against the £1.82/day run-rate when the data fills in.
- **Weekly vs daily is the right framing — and it barely matters here.** You're correct that the snapshot regenerates **weekly** (`seo-refresh.yml`, Mondays 04:00 UTC), so today's churn is not an ongoing daily cost. But the magnitude is so small that even a daily regen wouldn't register on this bill. The design deliberately keeps per-release cost at **zero** (CD reads the pre-built blob instead of calling the API every deploy — ADR 0031).

**Net effect on the forecast: £0.00/day added to run-rate.** The marketing websites are essentially free to run.

Cosmos is still **~75%** of the 30-day spend.

## Reconciliation (Pulumi vs reality)

Reconciled against live `az resource list` (now **31 resources** across `rg-town-crier-dev`/`-prod`/`-shared`, up from 29). The only delta vs the 06-18 snapshot is the **two new Storage Accounts**, both created by `f707f587` (#599) and Pulumi-managed (`createSeoSnapshotStorage` in `infra/`). No drift.

- **No orphans, no out-of-band creations.** Every billed resource maps to a Town Crier resource group and a known purpose.
- **`sttowncrierprod` exists but is empty — intentional, not waste.** The prod blob cutover is tag-gated and pending (tc-so05 / `project_seo_swa_decouple_blob`); the account was provisioned by the shared infra path so it's ready for the next prod release. Empty Hot storage with no transactions = £0. No action.
- **Untagged (5):** two managed certificates (`cert-api-dev`/`-prod`) and three Azure-auto-created monitor objects (Smart Detection, `azureapp-auto`, Service Health alert). All system-generated and free — expected.
- **Prior fixes still holding:** ACR at 71 MB / 10 GB (no overage), App* tables at 30-day retention, dev revision sprawl bounded by the weekly Monday cleanup job. No regressions.

---

## Recommendations

**No new recommendations this cycle.** Today's marketing-site work added no measurable cost (Storage at rest £0.00002/mo, transactions sub-penny, SWA Free SKU, render on free runners), so there is nothing new above the ~£2/month filing threshold. The two open beads from prior cycles carry forward unchanged.

### 1. Evaluate Cosmos cost reduction (throughput model + write efficiency)

- **Estimated saving:** ~£10–20/month (uncertain; carries throttling risk)
- **Bead:** `tc-tm5o` (open, P3)
- **Rationale:** Cosmos is **~67–75% of the bill** (~£31/mo of the last-30d £41) at ~50 RU/s and still drifting up, driven mainly by polling upserts against a pre-revenue user base. Two levers: a database-level **shared autoscale** throughput (needs a polling-burst profile first — spikes above the cap throttle, and the app has previously fought serverless 429s), or cutting redundant re-upserts of unchanged planning records each poll cycle (the more durable fix). Quantified-but-uncertain and risk-bearing against the go-live reliability posture, hence P3.

### 2. Fix the inline per-deploy revision deactivation for dev (Multiple mode)

- **Estimated saving:** ~£0/month (robustness, not cost)
- **Bead:** `tc-fxau` (open, P3)
- **Rationale:** Revision cleanup is already wired and working (prod `Single` mode auto-deactivates, weekly Monday job resets dev, `MaxInactiveRevisions=5` set). The narrow gap: `deploy-container-app`'s inline "deactivate old revision" step looks up the old revision via traffic `weight==100`, which is empty for dev's `latestRevision:true` traffic, so it silently no-ops every deploy — leaving the weekly job as the sole brake. Dev-only, low urgency.

### Noted but deliberately NOT recommended

- **Warm Go prod replica (`minReplicas=1`, ~£7.5/mo)** is the largest *controllable* line, but it is an accepted go-live decision to eliminate cold-start latency (`project_aca_min_replicas_decision`, 2026-06-13). Reverting trades UX for ~£7.5/mo against the pre-revenue reliability posture. Left as-is.
- **SEO snapshot Storage Accounts, SWA Free SKU, ACR Basic (£3.7/mo), Service Bus Basic (£0), Log Analytics (£0)** — all at or near their floors. No action.

---

## Methodology footnote

- **Actuals:** Cost Management query API, 60-day window (2026-04-23 → 2026-06-22), daily granularity, grouped by `ResourceId` (507 rows) and `ServiceName` (376 rows) via `scripts/fetch_costs.sh`.
- **Run-rate:** per-resource mean over the 7 clean days 2026-06-15 → 2026-06-21 (Go as sole API, .NET gone, daily totals £1.70–£1.93 → stable). 06-14 (cutover peak £2.20) and 06-22 (partial, £0.30 — current-day Azure data is always incomplete) both excluded.
- **Marketing-site cost:** verified bottom-up — `az storage account show` (SKU/tier/config) + `az monitor metrics list UsedCapacity` (1.34 MB dev, empty prod) + the `f707f587` diff (StorageV2/LRS/Hot, single container) + `seo-refresh.yml` cadence (`cron 0 4 * * 1`). Storage cost lines in the actuals read £0 so far.
- **Azure forecast source:** Cost Management forecast API, Custom timeframe, three windows from 2026-06-22 (rest-of-month £11.82, next-30d £40.55, next-quarter £123.72). Still a naive trailing-average extrapolation that cannot fit the mid-June step-change.
- **Pulumi reconciliation:** against `az resource list` ground truth (31 resources). The two new Storage Accounts are the only delta vs 06-18 and are Pulumi-claimed. No drift.
- **Exclusions / known gaps:** decommissioned .NET apps and one-off cutover/CI bursts excluded from forward projection. Next-quarter (£163.35) assumes flat run-rate plus mild Cosmos drift but **zero net-new user traffic** — real go-live adoption would push serverless Cosmos higher, so it's a **floor**, not a ceiling. Low confidence accordingly.
